skill.name: chatcrystal-debug-recall
skill.description: Recall ChatCrystal memories for debugging tasks involving failing tests, compiler errors, runtime exceptions, dependency issues, environment breakage, or performance regressions. Use when historical root causes, fixes, or pitfalls may accelerate diagnosis before proposing a fix.
skill.summary: Recall ChatCrystal memories for debugging tasks involving failing tests, compiler errors, runtime ex...
skill.author: ZengLiangYi

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by descriptive text, not cryptographic code. Static external-command findings are also false positives because the backticks are markdown inline code and a JSON example, not executable commands. The remaining concern is intentional sharing of local project paths, related files, branch names, and error signatures with the ChatCrystal recall MCP tool.

medium_findings.title: Debug Context Shared With Memory Recall Tool
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks the agent to call recall_for_task with project_dir, cwd, branch, related files, files_touched, and error_signatures when available. This is intended behavior, but it can disclose local project structure and failure details to ChatCrystal Core or its MCP storage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs use of recall_for_task and lists project context fields to include. This is not malicious by itself, but it creates a real data-minimization and privacy review concern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported As Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are markdown inline identifiers and a fenced JSON example. No shell, Ruby, or command execution syntax is present in the skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the backticks only format field names, tool names, and a JSON code block. There is no executable script or command invocation at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography lines contain metadata descriptions, debugging text, or ordinary words. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic algorithm usage appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic APIs or algorithm names in a code context. The pattern appears to be triggered by substrings inside normal descriptive text.

content.value_statement: Debugging often repeats past failures, fixes, and environment issues. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code query ChatCrystal memory before choosing a fix.
actual_capabilities: Triggers memory recall for failing tests, compiler errors, runtime exceptions, dependency issues, environment problems, and regressions.
Builds a debug-focused recall_for_task request with goal, task kind, project context, branch, files, and error signatures.
Prioritizes memories that describe root causes, remediations, and known pitfalls.
Supports degraded operation when ChatCrystal Core or the MCP tool is unavailable.
Reminds the agent to verify current failures instead of blindly applying recalled fixes.
limitations: Requires ChatCrystal Core and MCP access to recall_for_task for full functionality.
Does not diagnose or fix code without the agent performing normal debugging work.
May expose project paths, filenames, branch names, and error signatures to the memory system.
Can return no matches when relevant historical memories do not exist.

use_cases.title: Investigate recurring build failures
use_cases.description: Recall previous compiler errors and upgrade issues before changing build configuration.

use_cases.title: Triage production exceptions
use_cases.description: Use known exception signatures and past fixes to guide runtime debugging.

use_cases.title: Debug dependency regressions
use_cases.description: Find earlier dependency breakages and compatibility notes before proposing remediation.

prompt_templates.title: Recall a failing test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-debug-recall for this failing test. Include the test name, error message, current project directory, and related files if known.

prompt_templates.title: Check a compiler error
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-debug-recall before suggesting a fix for this compiler error. Focus on the error code, affected files, branch, and recent tool version changes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare runtime exceptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-debug-recall to search for similar runtime exceptions. Prioritize memories with matching stack traces, root causes, and verified remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a regression with context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-debug-recall with the debugging goal, branch, related files, files touched, package versions, and strongest error signatures. Summarize relevant memories, then verify against the current environment.

output_examples.input: A TypeScript upgrade caused TS2322 errors in the MCP server package.
output_examples.output: The agent recalls prior TypeScript upgrade fixes, highlights matching files and error signatures, and proposes checks before editing configuration.

output_examples.input: A service throws a runtime exception after a dependency update.
output_examples.output: The agent searches for similar dependency regressions, reports any matching historical causes, and continues normal debugging if no memory matches.

output_examples.input: ChatCrystal Core is unavailable during a failing test investigation.
output_examples.output: The agent states that long-term debug recall is unavailable and proceeds with standard local diagnosis.

best_practices: Provide precise error signatures, failing test names, and affected files.
Review recalled memories as hints, then verify each claim in the current project.
Avoid sending secrets or sensitive customer data in error context.
anti_patterns: Do not apply a recalled fix when the current failure signature differs materially.
Do not fabricate historical causes or fixes when the recall tool is unavailable.
Do not include unnecessary private paths, secrets, tokens, or full logs in recall input.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides the agent to query ChatCrystal memory for past debugging fixes, causes, and pitfalls.

faq.question: Does it fix bugs automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides historical context so the agent can debug with better evidence.

faq.question: What tools does it need?
faq.answer: Full mode requires ChatCrystal Core and MCP access to the recall_for_task tool.

faq.question: What happens if recall is unavailable?
faq.answer: The agent should continue debugging normally and briefly state that long-term recall is unavailable.

faq.question: Can it expose sensitive data?
faq.answer: It can send project paths, filenames, branch names, and error signatures to the memory system. Avoid including secrets.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a privacy warning. Static command and cryptography findings were false positives.
